habitat: Forest; forest edge; bamboo thickets; scrubland; and cultivated areas
story: Meet the Bornean Treepie (Dendrocitta cinerascens), a distinctive bird from the Corvidae family. Preferring habitats such as Forest; forest edge; bamboo thickets; scrubland; and cultivated areas, this species has adapted to thrive under specific environmental conditions. With an IUCN status of Least Concern (LC), its population trends are closely watched by scientists and enthusiasts. The Bornean Treepie serves as an indicator species for the health of its environment. Whether you are a seasoned ornithologist or a curious nature lover, learning about the Bornean Treepie enriches our understanding of nature.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
